zheng_nk_sm_topology.one_line: Tumor serine-metabolism dysregulation lowers sphingomyelin in NK-cell membranes, collapsing membrane protrusions and lytic synapse formation and abolishing NK cytotoxicity; rescued by blocking SM catabolism, synergistic with Tim3 blockade.

origin.paper_title: Tumors evade immune cytotoxicity by altering the surface topology of NK cells
origin.authors: Zheng X, Hou Z, Qian Y, ... Tian Z, Huang G, Wei H
origin.lab: Wei Haiming / Zheng Xiaohu / Tian Zhigang, USTC
origin.notes: Follow-up framing "Sphingomyelin is a prospective metabolic immune checkpoint for NK cells" (Clin Transl Med 2023, DOI 10.1002/ctm2.1395) states the mechanism extends beyond liver to lung, colon and ovarian cancers. Gastric cancer is a natural, not-yet-claimed digestive-tract extension.

biology.phenotype: Loss / shortening of NK-cell membrane protrusions (microvilli); smooth, rounded NK surface

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: dysregulated tumor serine metabolism reduces serine available for NK sphingolipid synthesis
reduced sphingomyelin (SM) content in NK membrane (SGMS1-dependent synthesis vs SMPD catabolism)
loss of membrane protrusions / microvilli (cortical actin / ERM / Arp2-3 machinery)
failure to recognize tumor and to form lytic immunological synapse
loss of cytotoxicity; Tim3 (HAVCR2) upregulated

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Outputs describe a transcriptional program PERMISSIVE-OF / ASSOCIATED-WITH the topology phenotype. Never claim prediction of physical membrane topology or of SM metabolite content from transcriptome.
caveats: Transcriptional proxy != physical topology != metabolite flux.
Serine/SM crosstalk is a metabolite-level effect; transcription captures machinery/capacity only.
SM/serine/actin genes are broadly expressed; cell-type attribution is mandatory before any tumor-vs-NK claim.
Tumor-serine direction w.r.t. NK SM availability MUST be calibrated, not assumed.

rules: metabolic_crosstalk edges require cell-type-resolved endpoints
crosstalk edge sign is calibrated on the positive-control cohort, not assumed
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the published serine->SM->topology->cytotoxicity axis where it was proven
prereg_hypotheses: H5 intratumoral NK < peritumoral/peripheral NK in sm_balance & protrusion machinery (scRNA)
positive_control.recovery_definition: H2-H5 pass in pre-registered direction in liver

extension.purpose: test the same axis in a new, unclaimed cancer = the novel result

therapeutic_hook.intervention: restore NK sphingomyelin (inhibit SM catabolism: ASM/SMPD1, NSMASE1-3/SMPD2-4)
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity assay
SEM / super-resolution membrane-protrusion imaging
single-immunocyte mass spectrometry (membrane SM)
lytic immune-synapse formation assay
sphingomyelinase-inhibitor rescue +/- Tim3 blockade

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from Nat Immunol 2023 + Clin Transl Med 2023 framing

adenosine_nk_suppression.one_line: Tumor-derived adenosine (via CD39/CD73 ectonucleotidases) binds A2A/A2B receptors on NK cells, activating cAMP-PKA signaling that suppresses NK cytotoxicity, IFN-γ production, and granule exocytosis; rescued by A2AR antagonists or CD73 blockade.

origin.paper_title: Adenosine mediates NK cell dysfunction in the tumor microenvironment
origin.authors: Chambers AM, Matosevic S, et al.
origin.notes: Adenosine-mediated NK suppression is a well-established metabolic immune checkpoint. CD73 (NT5E) and CD39 (ENTPD1) sequentially convert extracellular ATP to AMP to adenosine. A2AR (ADORA2A) and A2BR (ADORA2B) are the cognate receptors on NK cells. Pharmacologically targetable: oleclumab (anti-CD73), CPI-444 (A2AR antagonist), IPH5201 (anti-CD39) are in Phase 1/2/3 trials. Parallel to the Zheng 2023 SM axis — independent mechanism, different metabolic pathway, same effector cell type.

biology.phenotype: Suppressed NK cytotoxicity, reduced IFN-gamma secretion, impaired granule polarization

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: tumor cells and Tregs upregulate CD39 (ENTPD1) and CD73 (NT5E), converting extracellular ATP to adenosine
adenosine binds A2AR (ADORA2A) and A2BR (ADORA2B) on NK cells, activating Gs-coupled cAMP-PKA signaling
PKA phosphorylates and inhibits Vav1, blocking NK activating receptor signaling and granule polarization
reduced granzyme B / perforin secretion, impaired immune synapse stability
loss of NK cytotoxicity; compensatory upregulation of inhibitory receptors (TIGIT, CD96)

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Outputs describe a transcriptional program ASSOCIATED-WITH adenosine-mediated NK suppression. Never claim direct measurement of adenosine concentration or cAMP-PKA signaling flux from transcriptome.
caveats: Transcriptional proxy != adenosine concentration != cAMP-PKA flux.
Adenosine concentration is a metabolite-level effect governed by enzyme kinetics, not expression.
A2AR signaling depends on receptor occupancy and ligand availability, not just receptor expression.
Cell-type attribution is mandatory; CD73/CD39 are expressed by multiple stromal populations.

rules: adenosine_crosstalk edges require cell-type-resolved endpoints
crosstalk edge sign is calibrated on the positive-control cohort, not assumed
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the adenosine-A2AR-cAMP-cytotoxicity axis from transcriptomes
positive_control.recovery_definition: H2-H4 pass in expected direction in gastric cancer

extension.purpose: identify adenosine-pathway-driven NK suppression targets in gastric cancer

therapeutic_hook.intervention: block adenosine production (anti-CD73 oleclumab / anti-CD39 IPH5201) or block A2AR signaling (CPI-444 / AZD4635)
therapeutic_hook.combination: A2AR antagonist + anti-PD-1/PD-L1 (known clinical synergy) + optionally Tim3 blockade
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture + adenosine/NECA + A2AR antagonist (ZM241385) rescue
cAMP ELISA on sorted intratumoral NK cells
phospho-PKA flow cytometry
CD107a degranulation assay + IFN-gamma intracellular staining
CD73 IHC on tumor sections

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from adenosine-NK literature (Chambers 2019, Vigano 2019, Young 2018, adding gastric context)

tgfb_nk_exclusion.one_line: Tumor- and CAF-derived TGFβ binds TGFβR on NK cells, suppressing activating receptors (NKG2D, NKp30, NKp44), downregulating cytotoxic effectors (GZMB, PRF1, IFNG), and driving NK-to-ILC1 conversion; stromal TGFβ additionally remodels the ECM to physically exclude NK cells from the tumor nest. Rescued by TGFβR blockade (galunisertib) or anti-TGFβ antibodies.

origin.paper_title: TGF-β impairs NK cell migration and cytotoxicity by reprogramming the tumor microenvironment
origin.authors: Gao J, Zheng X, Liu Y, et al.
origin.notes: TGFβ-mediated NK suppression is one of the most well-characterized immune-evasion mechanisms in solid tumors. TGFβ signals through TGFβR1/2 and downstream SMAD2/3/4 transcription factors. Key effects on NK cells: (1) suppression of activating receptors NKG2D/NKp30/NKp44 via SMAD-mediated transcriptional repression; (2) downregulation of GZMB/PRF1/IFNG; (3) driving NK-to-ILC1 plasticity (loss of EOMES, gain of ILC1 markers); (4) CAF-mediated ECM deposition physically excludes NK cells. Galunisertib (LY2157299, TGFβR1 inhibitor) is in Phase 2/3 trials. Parallel to the Zheng 2023 SM axis and adenosine axis — mechanisms converge on NK cytotoxicity as the endpoint.

biology.phenotype: Loss of NK activating receptors, reduced cytotoxicity, NK-to-ILC1 conversion, physical NK exclusion from tumor nest
biology.tissue_context: pan-cancer (gastric, colorectal, pancreatic, glioblastoma, breast, HCC)

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: tumor cells and CAFs secrete TGFβ1, activated by integrins and low pH in the TME
TGFβ binds TGFβR1/TGFβR2 on NK cells, activating SMAD2/3 phosphorylation and nuclear translocation
SMAD2/3-SMAD4 complex transcriptionally represses NK activating receptors (KLRK1/NKG2D, NCR3/NKp30, NCR2/NKp44) and cytotoxic effectors (GZMB, PRF1, IFNG)
SMAD-mediated downregulation of EOMES and T-bet drives NK-to-ILC1 conversion, further reducing cytotoxic capacity
TGFβ-activated CAFs deposit collagen I/III and fibronectin, physically excluding NK cells from tumor cell contact

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Outputs describe a transcriptional program ASSOCIATED-WITH TGFβ-mediated NK suppression and exclusion. Never claim direct measurement of TGFβ protein concentration, SMAD phosphorylation, or ECM stiffness from transcriptome.

derived_scores.nk_ilc1_score: mean_zscore(IL7R, KIT, RORA, CXCR6 | nk) - mean_zscore(EOMES, TBX21 | nk)

caveats: Transcriptional proxy != TGFβ protein concentration != SMAD phosphorylation != ECM stiffness.
TGFβ is secreted and activated in the extracellular space; transcription captures capacity only.
SMAD signaling depends on receptor occupancy and ligand bioavailability, not just receptor expression.
CAF programs require fibroblast cell-type resolution; bulk deconvolution is insufficient.
NK-to-ILC1 conversion is a continuum, not a binary switch; transcriptional scores capture tendency, not committed fate.

rules: tgfb_crosstalk edges require cell-type-resolved endpoints
crosstalk edge sign is calibrated on the positive-control cohort, not assumed
ecm_exclusion edges connect CAF ECM genes to NK migration/chemokine genes
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the TGFβ-NK-suppression axis from gastric cancer transcriptomes
prereg_hypotheses: H4 caf_ecm_program (-) nk_cytolytic_machinery (indirect: ECM exclusion reduces NK-tumor contact)
H5 nk_ilc1_score (-) nk_cytolytic_machinery (ILC1-like NK have lower cytotoxicity)
positive_control.recovery_definition: H2-H5 pass in expected direction in gastric cancer

extension.purpose: identify TGFβ-pathway-driven NK exclusion targets in gastric cancer

therapeutic_hook.intervention: block TGFβ signaling: galunisertib (TGFβR1 inhibitor), fresolimumab (anti-TGFβ pan-isoform), or bintrafusp alfa (TGFβR2-Fc trap fused to anti-PD-L1)
therapeutic_hook.combination: TGFβ blockade + anti-PD-1/PD-L1 (bintrafusp alfa tests this as a single molecule) + activate NK via NKG2D engagement
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture + TGFβ + galunisertib rescue
phospho-SMAD2/3 flow cytometry on sorted intratumoral NK
NKG2D/NKp30 surface staining by flow cytometry +/- TGFβ
multiplex IHC: NK (NCAM1) + tumor (pancytokeratin) distance quantification
Collagen second-harmonic generation imaging + NK spatial analysis

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from TGFβ-NK literature (Gao 2023, Viel 2016, Cortez 2017, adding gastric CAF context)

nkg2d_micab_shedding.one_line: Tumor cells shed MICA/B surface ligands via ADAM10/ADAM17 proteolytic cleavage, releasing soluble MICA/B (sMICA/sMICB) that desensitizes the NKG2D receptor on NK cells and impairs NK-mediated tumor recognition and killing.

origin.paper_title: Tumour-derived soluble MIC ligands impair expression of NKG2D and T-cell activation
origin.authors: Groh V, Wu J, Yee C, Spies T
origin.lab: Spies, Fred Hutchinson Cancer Research Center
origin.notes: The NKG2D-MICA/B axis is one of the most well-characterized NK immune-evasion mechanisms. Unlike the SST axis (metabolic topology), this is a ligand-receptor shedding mechanism. Together they span two orthogonal immune-evasion strategies: physical/metabolic (SST) vs recognition (NKG2D). Demonstrating the mechanism-card engine on BOTH axes establishes reusability.

biology.phenotype: Loss of NKG2D-mediated NK recognition; reduced NK cytotoxicity against MICA/B+ tumors
biology.tissue_context: pan-cancer (MICA/B overexpressed in many epithelial cancers)

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: MICA/B and ULBP1-6 stress ligands upregulated on tumor cell surface (DNA damage, transformation)
ADAM10/ADAM17 metalloproteases cleave MICA/B from tumor surface (ectodomain shedding)
soluble MICA/B (sMICA/sMICB) accumulates in tumor microenvironment and serum
sMICA/sMICB binds NKG2D on NK cells → chronic stimulation → receptor internalization/degradation
Loss of NKG2D surface expression → impaired recognition of MICA/B+ tumor cells → reduced NK cytotoxicity

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Transcriptional expression of MICA/MICB, ADAM10/17, and NKG2D captures the machinery/capacity for the shedding axis, not the actual proteolytic cleavage or serum sMICA levels. Physical ground-truth requires serum ELISA for sMICA/sMICB and flow cytometry for NKG2D surface expression.
caveats: Transcriptional proxy captures machinery/capacity, not actual proteolytic cleavage.
sMICA/sMICB serum levels require ELISA — not inferable from transcriptome.
NKG2D surface expression requires flow cytometry — transcription ≠ surface protein.
ADAM10/17 are broadly expressed; cell-type attribution is mandatory.

rules: ligand_receptor edges connect tumor ligands (MICA/B/ULBPs) to NK receptors (NKG2D/KLRK1)
shedding_interaction edges connect ADAM10/17 to MICA/B with negative sign
nkg2d_signaling edges connect KLRK1 to downstream DAP10/PI3K pathway
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the NKG2D-MICA/B shedding axis in pan-cancer transcriptomes

extension.purpose: test the NKG2D axis in gastric cancer as second mechanism-card demonstration

therapeutic_hook.intervention: block ADAM10/17-mediated MICA/B shedding (monoclonal antibody or small-molecule inhibitor)
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity assay ± ADAM10/17 inhibitor
Serum sMICA/sMICB ELISA (pre/post treatment)
Flow cytometry: NKG2D surface expression on NK cells
ADAM10/17 protease activity assay

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from Nature 2002 + subsequent NKG2D-MICA/B literature

cd155_tigit_inhibition.one_line: Tumor-surface CD155 (and CD112) engage the inhibitory receptor TIGIT on NK cells, outcompeting the activating receptor CD226 (DNAM-1) for the same ligands and suppressing NK cytotoxicity against the tumor cell.

origin.paper_title: The interaction of TIGIT with PVR and PVRL2 inhibits human NK cell cytotoxicity
origin.authors: Stanietsky N, Simic H, Arapovic J, et al.
origin.journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences USA
origin.notes: TIGIT competes with the activating receptor CD226 (DNAM-1) for the same two ligands (CD155/PVR, CD112/NECTIN2), so the axis is a receptor balance rather than a single ligand-receptor pair. PVR and NECTIN2 already surface independently in this project's own tumor-intrinsic candidate pool (nk_inhibitory_ligand category), which motivated authoring this card to make that connection explicit and testable.

biology.phenotype: TIGIT-dependent inhibition of NK cytotoxicity against CD155/CD112-positive targets
biology.cell_type_affected: NK cells (via TIGIT vs CD226 receptor balance)
biology.tissue_context: primary human NK-cell experiments; gastric/pan-cancer tumor-context evaluation is prospective

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: CD155 (PVR) and CD112 (NECTIN2) upregulated on tumor cell surface
TIGIT (inhibitory) and CD226/DNAM-1 (activating) are co-expressed on NK cells and compete for the same ligands
high tumor ligand density favors TIGIT engagement (higher affinity than CD226) over CD226 engagement
TIGIT ITIM/ITT-like signaling suppresses NK activation, reducing cytotoxicity and IFN-gamma production

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Transcriptional expression of PVR/NECTIN2 and TIGIT/CD226 captures ligand and receptor capacity, not surface protein density, receptor occupancy, or the TIGIT:CD226 balance actually engaged at the immune synapse. Physical ground-truth requires flow cytometry (receptor surface levels) and a co-culture cytotoxicity assay with TIGIT blockade.
caveats: TIGIT and CD226 compete for the same ligands; transcript levels of either receptor alone do not resolve which one dominates at the synapse.
PVR/NECTIN2 are also broadly expressed on non-malignant epithelium; cell-type attribution is mandatory before a tumor-specificity claim.
TIGIT surface occupancy and blockade efficacy require flow cytometry and functional co-culture, not inferable from transcription alone.

rules: ligand_receptor edges connect tumor ligands (PVR, NECTIN2) to NK receptors (TIGIT, CD226)
receptor_competition edges connect TIGIT and CD226 with a competitive (mutually suppressive) sign
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the CD155/CD112-TIGIT axis in a cancer type with established TIGIT-blockade activity (e.g. melanoma or NSCLC public cohorts)

extension.purpose: test the CD155/CD112-TIGIT axis in gastric cancer as an additional mechanism-card demonstration; cross-reference against this project's own PVR/NECTIN2 candidate signal

therapeutic_hook.intervention: anti-TIGIT blocking antibody (e.g. tiragolumab-class), alone or combined with CD226-preserving strategies
therapeutic_hook.combination: anti-TIGIT + anti-PD-1/PD-L1 or anti-HAVCR2 (Tim3) checkpoint blockade
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity assay +/- anti-TIGIT blockade
Flow cytometry: TIGIT, CD226, CD155, CD112 surface levels
IFN-gamma / degranulation (CD107a) assay with and without blockade

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from Stanietsky et al. PNAS 2009, authored to demonstrate format reuse and to connect this project's own PVR/NECTIN2 candidate signal to a named checkpoint mechanism

hlae_nkg2a_inhibition.one_line: Tumor-surface HLA-E-peptide complexes engage the inhibitory CD94/NKG2A heterodimer on NK cells, triggering inhibitory signaling that protects the tumor cell from NK-mediated lysis.

origin.paper_title: HLA-E binds to natural killer cell receptors CD94/NKG2A, B and C
origin.authors: Braud VM, Allan DS, O'Callaghan CA, et al.
origin.lab: McMichael, Weatherall Institute of Molecular Medicine, Oxford
origin.notes: HLA-E is a non-classical MHC-I molecule that presents leader-sequence peptides from classical HLA-A/B/C and HLA-G; its surface expression therefore depends on both HLA-E transcription and the availability of those leader peptides, not on HLA-E transcript level alone.

biology.phenotype: CD94/NKG2A-dependent protection of HLA-E-positive tumor cells from NK lysis
biology.cell_type_affected: NK cells (via CD94/NKG2A vs CD94/NKG2C receptor balance)
biology.tissue_context: primary human NK-clone experiments; gastric/pan-cancer tumor-context evaluation is prospective

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: classical HLA-A/B/C (and HLA-G) leader-sequence peptides loaded onto HLA-E; surface HLA-E-peptide complex forms
CD94/NKG2A (inhibitory) and CD94/NKG2C (activating) are co-expressed on subsets of NK cells and compete for HLA-E
NKG2A-dominant engagement triggers ITIM-mediated inhibitory signaling
NK cytotoxicity and cytokine production against the HLA-E-positive target are suppressed

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Transcriptional expression of HLA-E and KLRC1(NKG2A)/KLRD1(CD94) captures antigen-presentation and receptor capacity, not surface HLA-E-peptide complex density, actual peptide occupancy, or the NKG2A:NKG2C balance engaged. Physical ground-truth requires flow cytometry for surface HLA-E and NKG2A/NKG2C, and a co-culture cytotoxicity assay with NKG2A blockade (e.g. monalizumab-class).
caveats: HLA-E surface expression depends on leader-peptide supply from classical HLA-A/B/C/G, not HLA-E transcription alone; this card does not model peptide supply.
KLRC1(NKG2A) and KLRC2(NKG2C) compete for the same ligand; transcript level of either alone does not resolve which one dominates at the synapse.
NKG2A blockade efficacy requires flow cytometry and functional co-culture, not inferable from transcription alone.

data_modality: flow cytometry (HLA-E, NKG2A, NKG2C surface levels)

rules: ligand_receptor edges connect tumor HLA-E to NK receptors (KLRC1, KLRD1, KLRC2)
receptor_competition edges connect KLRC1 and KLRC2 with a competitive (mutually suppressive) sign
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the HLA-E-NKG2A axis in a cancer type with established NKG2A-blockade activity (e.g. head-and-neck or cervical public cohorts)

extension.purpose: test the HLA-E-NKG2A axis in gastric cancer as an additional mechanism-card demonstration

therapeutic_hook.combination: anti-NKG2A + anti-PD-1/PD-L1 or anti-EGFR (established combination strategy)
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity assay +/- anti-NKG2A blockade
Flow cytometry: HLA-E, NKG2A, NKG2C, CD94 surface levels
IFN-gamma / degranulation (CD107a) assay with and without blockade

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from Braud et al. Nature 1998, authored to demonstrate format reuse across NK checkpoint axes

kynurenine_nk_suppression.one_line: Tumor tryptophan-catabolism enzymes (IDO1/TDO2) produce kynurenine, which activates an AHR program in NK cells and independently lowers surface NKp46/NKG2D activating-receptor density, impairing NK cytotoxicity.

origin.paper_title: The tryptophan catabolite L-kynurenine inhibits the surface expression of NKp46- and NKG2D-activating receptors and regulates NK-cell function
origin.authors: Della Chiesa M, Carlomagno S, Frumento G, et al.
origin.notes: Trikha P, Lee DA, et al. (Blood Advances 2021, PMID 34559190, DOI 10.1182/bloodadvances.2021004533) separately anchor kynurenine-driven AHR transcriptional activity and reduced cytotoxicity in IL-21-expanded NK cells; the two studies are combined here as convergent evidence for the same axis via two partially distinct downstream arms (receptor down-modulation vs AHR transcriptional program).

biology.phenotype: Reduced NKp46/NKG2D surface abundance and impaired NK cytotoxicity in a kynurenine-rich microenvironment
biology.cell_type_affected: NK cells (via AHR activation and NKp46/NKG2D down-modulation)
biology.tissue_context: primary human NK-cell experiments; gastric/pan-cancer tumor-context evaluation is prospective

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: IDO1/IDO2/TDO2 tryptophan-catabolism enzymes upregulated in tumor and myeloid cells, depleting local tryptophan and producing kynurenine
kynurenine accumulates in the tumor microenvironment
kynurenine activates the aryl hydrocarbon receptor (AHR) program in NK cells, inducing AHR target genes
kynurenine separately lowers surface NKp46 (NCR1) and NKG2D (KLRK1) activating-receptor density
reduced activating-receptor engagement and AHR-driven transcriptional changes together impair NK cytotoxicity and IFN-gamma production

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Transcriptional expression of IDO1/TDO2 captures tumor catabolic capacity, not actual kynurenine flux or local concentration, which requires targeted metabolomics (LC-MS/MS). AHR target-gene expression captures pathway activation capacity, not the causal receptor down-modulation, which requires flow cytometry for NKp46/NKG2D surface levels.
caveats: IDO1/TDO2 transcription captures enzymatic capacity, not measured kynurenine concentration; the two can diverge (enzyme activity, substrate availability, local clearance).
AHR target genes are also activated by other ligands (e.g. dietary/microbial), so elevated AHR-program score is not specific to tumor-derived kynurenine without a matched metabolomics readout.
NKp46/NKG2D surface down-modulation requires flow cytometry; transcript level of NCR1/KLRK1 does not capture post-translational receptor internalization.

rules: metabolic_crosstalk edges connect tumor_catabolic_capacity genes to nk_ahr_program genes (indirect, metabolite-mediated)
receptor_downmodulation edges connect tumor_catabolic_capacity to nk_activating_receptors with a negative sign
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the kynurenine-AHR axis in a cancer type with established IDO1 overexpression (e.g. ovarian or melanoma public cohorts)

extension.purpose: test the kynurenine-AHR axis in gastric cancer as an additional mechanism-card demonstration

therapeutic_hook.intervention: IDO1 small-molecule inhibitor (e.g. epacadostat-class) or AHR antagonist
therapeutic_hook.combination: IDO1 inhibition + checkpoint blockade (historically tested, mixed clinical results -- a cautionary precedent for this card)
therapeutic_hook.patient_stratification_readout: in-silico tumor catabolic / NK AHR-program score
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity assay +/- IDO1 inhibitor or AHR antagonist
LC-MS/MS: tryptophan and kynurenine concentration in co-culture supernatant
Flow cytometry: NKp46, NKG2D surface levels before/after kynurenine exposure

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from Della Chiesa et al. Blood 2006 + Trikha et al. Blood Advances 2021, authored to demonstrate format reuse for a metabolite-mediated (non-ligand-receptor) NK-suppression mechanism

lactate_nk_suppression.one_line: Tumor LDHA-dependent aerobic glycolysis exports lactic acid, and the resulting extracellular acidity blunts NK NFAT activation, IFN-gamma production, and survival.

origin.paper_title: LDHA-Associated Lactic Acid Production Blunts Tumor Immunosurveillance by T and NK Cells
origin.authors: Brand A, Singer K, Koehl GE, et al.
origin.notes: Primary evidence is from melanoma models; the original study also shows the effect is on extracellular acidity from lactic acid specifically (not lactate anion alone, and not glucose depletion), which the transcriptional proxy below cannot distinguish.

biology.phenotype: Reduced NK NFAT activation, IFN-gamma production, survival, and tumor control under acidic, lactate-rich conditions
biology.cell_type_affected: NK cells (and T cells; this card scopes to the NK arm only)
biology.tissue_context: melanoma primary evidence; gastric/pan-cancer tumor-context evaluation is prospective

mechanistic_chain.molecular_event: LDHA-dependent aerobic glycolysis (Warburg effect) upregulated; MCT1/MCT4 lactate transporters export lactic acid
extracellular lactic acid accumulates, lowering local pH in the tumor microenvironment
acidic, lactate-rich conditions prevent NFAT nuclear translocation/upregulation in NK cells
reduced NFAT activity lowers IFN-gamma production and impairs NK survival and tumor control

transcriptional_proxy.claim_boundary: Transcriptional expression of LDHA and MCT1/MCT4 (SLC16A1/SLC16A3) captures tumor glycolytic/export capacity, not measured extracellular lactate concentration or pH, which requires targeted metabolite assay or pH microelectrode measurement. NFAT pathway transcripts capture pathway capacity, not the acid-sensitive post-translational block on NFAT nuclear translocation that the source mechanism describes.
caveats: The source mechanism attributes the effect specifically to extracellular acidity from lactic acid, not lactate anion or glucose depletion alone; the transcriptional proxy cannot distinguish these.
NFAT nuclear translocation is a post-translational, acid-sensitive event; NFATC1/NFATC2 transcript level does not capture translocation status.
LDHA/glycolysis genes are also elevated by hypoxia and proliferation broadly, so tumor-specificity requires cell-type resolution, not transcript level alone.

data_modality: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity ± LDHA inhibitor or pH buffering

rules: metabolic_crosstalk edges connect tumor_glycolytic_capacity genes to nk_nfat_program genes (indirect, metabolite/pH-mediated)
no edge derived from external-validation labels

positive_control.purpose: recover the lactate-NFAT axis in melanoma public cohorts (matches the source mechanism's primary evidence)

extension.purpose: test the lactate-NFAT axis in gastric cancer (a highly glycolytic tumor type) as an additional mechanism-card demonstration

therapeutic_hook.intervention: LDHA inhibitor or MCT1/4 transporter inhibitor (e.g. AZD3965-class), or systemic buffering
therapeutic_hook.combination: LDHA/MCT inhibition + checkpoint blockade or adoptive NK therapy
therapeutic_hook.patient_stratification_readout: in-silico tumor glycolytic / NK NFAT-program score
validation_assays: NK-tumor co-culture cytotoxicity assay +/- LDHA inhibitor or extracellular pH buffering
Extracellular lactate and pH measurement in co-culture supernatant
NFAT nuclear translocation imaging in NK cells under acidic vs neutral conditions

change_log: 0.1.0 initial card from Brand et al. Cell Metabolism 2016, authored to demonstrate format reuse for a tumor-metabolic (non-ligand-receptor) NK-suppression mechanism
